Section 1

Overview

A behavior-informed microsimulation that predicts how freight shifts between truck, barge, and rail under different cost, time, reliability, and policy conditions — and reports the result as sensitivity / tipping-point curves, not a single forecast.

AVAILABLE implemented & runs today PLANNED near-term, interface specified FUNDED-WORK needs restricted data / external integration

What the tool does

For a population of individual shipments on a corridor, it computes the objective cost and time of each modal alternative (truck-only, truck→hub→barge, truck→hub→rail), injects supply-side reliability (terminal-queue congestion and lock/river-event delay), evaluates a behavioral generalized-cost choice model, and sweeps the uncertain parameters to map the conditions under which each mode wins. It answers two policy questions:

  • Forward (RQ1): given conditions X, what is the modal split — by commodity, by corridor, with uncertainty bands?
  • Inverse (RQ2): what is the least intervention (subsidy, hub investment, reliability improvement) needed to move a commodity from truck to barge/rail — and where is that simply infeasible?

The five-phase pipeline

PhaseDoesOutput
1 Shipment generationdisaggregated OD flows → population of shipment agentsshipments.parquet
2 Network skimcost & time for all three modes per OD pairskim.parquet
2.5 Reliabilityterminal-queue + lock/river delay → transit mean & SDreliability.parquet
3 Utilitygeneralized-cost utility per shipment × modeutility.parquet
4 Mode choicelogit probability + Monte-Carlo assignmentmode_share.parquet
5 Sensitivity sweepre-run over a parameter grid → tipping curvessweep_results.parquet
6 Policy leversminimum-lever & feasibility per commoditymin_lever_table.parquet

One tool, two fidelity levels

The same code runs a fast state-of-practice (SOP) screening profile (open data, laptop) or a richer state-of-the-art (SOTA) research profile (survey data, cluster), selected by one config knob. See Fidelity profiles.

Status today: the model core, both corridors (Lower Mississippi + Tennessee River), all five phases, the policy-lever solvers, and the SOP/SOTA switch are AVAILABLE and pass 108 unit tests. The demo runs on synthetic-but-realistic data; loading your own real data is PLANNED (see Roadmap).
Section 2

Getting started

Install, run the end-to-end demo, find the outputs.

Requirements

Pure Python, single machine, no Spark/Databricks. Python ≥ 3.10.

# core
numpy>=1.26   pandas>=2.1   scipy>=1.11   networkx>=3.2
pyarrow>=14   pyyaml>=6     matplotlib>=3.8
# optional geo stack — only for real-data Phase 2 / corridor maps
geopandas>=0.14   shapely>=2.0
pip install -r requirements.txt

Run the demo

python run_demo.py                # full pipeline + figures
python run_demo.py --skip-figures # parquet outputs only
python run_demo.py --figures-only # re-render figures from existing sweep_results

The run logs each phase and finishes in seconds on the demo population. It executes Phase 1 → 2 → 2.5 → 3 → 4 → 5, then the Phase 6 policy-lever tables and the hero figures.

Where things land

PathContents
config/parameters.yaml, scenarios.yaml, sweep.yaml, commodity_table.csv
data/raw/input demand file (demo: sample_disagg_outputs.csv)
data/processed/shipments, skim, reliability (intermediates)
data/outputs/mode_share, mode_share_by_commodity, sweep_results, min_lever_table, min_effort_levers, utility, choices
figures/rendered PNGs

Run the tests

python -m unittest discover -s tests   # 108 tests
Section 3

How it works

The behavioral core in plain language. Full derivation: ALGORITHM.md and the white paper.

Generalized cost, then a logit

Every modal alternative k for a shipment s is scored by a single generalized cost in dollars. Utility is its negative; a logit with an explicit scale θ turns dollar differences into choice probabilities.

GCₖ = transport_costₖ + vot_driver_per_hr × driver_timeₖ // tractor engaged: full trip (truck); drayage legs only (barge/rail) + (value × holding_rate/8760) × total_timeₖ // cargo inventory carrying cost, all modes + vor_ratio × (value × holding_rate/8760) × reliabilityₖ // value of reliability, as a ratio − ASCₖ // residual unobserved preference Uₖ = −GCₖ Pₖ = softmax( Uₖ / θ ) over available modes

Why behavior emerges without tuning

Modal outcomes fall out of cargo value density, not fitted constants:

  • Bulk (grain, minerals, chemicals; low $/ton): tiny inventory + reliability cost → tolerates slow barge → barge/rail compete on cost.
  • Manufactured (high $/ton): large inventory + reliability cost, high container lift fees → truck wins.

The logit scale θ

θ (dollars per util, default 300) is the one parameter a borrowed logit cannot transfer — it sets how deterministic choices are. It is an explicit swept assumption, not a tuned constant. Small θ → near-deterministic (argmax); large θ → probabilities spread out. At ~$300 a $300 cost gap gives roughly a 73/27 split.

Supply → demand reliability coupling

The distinguishing feature. Phase 2.5 produces a transit-time mean and standard deviation for barge from a terminal queue (an analytic M/M/c model of the transshipment hub) and/or lock/river-event delay distributions. Those feed two utility terms: a value-scaled cargo reliability cost and a value-independent supply-delay cost (the vessel/operator time a delay ties up). The value-independent term is what lets a hub-investment or lock-improvement lever move even low-value bulk barge.

The utility hierarchy (M1–M4)

LevelAddsProfile
M1core: cost + driver VOT + cargo inventory + cargo reliability + supply-delay + single-per-mode ASC—
M2+ carrier reliability term (γ·v·σ)SOP (practice)
M3+ commodity-specific ASCsSOTA
M4+ shipment-size economy term on barge/railSOTA (art)

A mixed/random-coefficient logit (often called M6) is available as a choice_model option for taste heterogeneity.

Section 4

The pipeline, phase by phase

Purpose, inputs, outputs, and key parameters for each phase. AVAILABLE unless marked.

Phase 1 — Shipment generation

Purpose: turn disaggregated zone-to-zone commodity flows into a population of discrete shipment agents.

Inputs: a disaggregated OD file (origin_taz, destination_taz, sctg2, annual_tons) and commodity_table.csv (per-band payload, value density, time-sensitivity).

Process: for each (OD, commodity) the annual tonnage is divided by the band payload to get a shipment count; each agent is materialized with jittered payload and value density (deterministic from the RNG seed).

Output — shipments.parquet: shipment_id, origin_taz, destination_taz, sctg2, commodity_band, time_sensitivity, payload_tons, value_density_per_ton, value_total.

The demo input is a small synthetic stand-in. Loading a real fdt/Transearch disaggregate file is the Phase-1 ingest adapter PLANNED — see Data & inputs.

Phase 2 — Multimodal network skim

Purpose: compute cost and time for truck-only, truck→barge, and truck→rail for every OD pair.

Inputs: shipments; per-mile rates, lift fees, speeds, and hub dwells from parameters.yaml → phase2; a corridor geography.

Process: drayage + line-haul + transfer for each chained alternative; records the lock/event sequence per barge route. Mode availability is suppressed beyond drayage-distance thresholds (barge 150 mi, rail 75 mi).

Output — skim.parquet: per (OD, mode): distance, time, and a fixed/per-ton cost split.

Networks today: synthetic stylized geography AVAILABLE. Real BTS NTAD / USACE shapefile readers PLANNED (load_real_*_graph).

Phase 2.5 — Supply-side reliability

Purpose: convert congestion and disruption into a barge transit-time mean and standard deviation per route per scenario — the supply→demand coupling.

Mechanisms (phase25.reliability_mechanism):

  • terminal_queue (default) — analytic M/M/c queue at the hub; congestion wait Wℚ adds to transit and its SD feeds reliability. Capacity (servers, service rate) is the state-actionable investment lever.
  • river_events — lognormal low-water / fog / tow events (Lower Mississippi) or lock-queue delays (Tennessee River), sampled per Monte-Carlo lockage run.
  • both — queue + events (means add, variances add).

Output — reliability.parquet: per (barge route, scenario): expected delay, time_barge_mean_hr, time_barge_sd_hr, and a 95th-pct buffer.

Fitting real distributions from raw USACE LPMS records is FUNDED-WORK; the demo uses published-literature distributions.

Phase 3 — Behavioral utility

Purpose: compute the generalized-cost utility of each alternative per shipment.

Inputs: shipments + skim + reliability; the behavioral parameters in phase3 (VOT, inventory holding rate, VOR ratio, supply-delay cost, ASCs, θ).

Process: the generalized-cost equation in How it works, evaluated under each VOR anchor (NCHRP 824 high / Shams 2017 low). The utility hierarchy term set is selected by the fidelity utility_spec (M1–M4).

Output — utility.parquet: per (shipment, scenario, anchor, mode): utility and availability.

Phase 4 — Mode choice

Purpose: turn utilities into probabilities and discrete choices.

Process: Pₖ = softmax(Uₖ/θ) over available modes; Monte-Carlo assignment (default 100 runs) produces variance bands at the central scenarios. The Phase 5 sweep uses the analytic shares directly (no sampling noise).

Outputs: mode_share.parquet (per scenario×anchor×mode: share_mean, share_std, ton_share, value_share) and mode_share_by_commodity.parquet; choices.parquet at central scenarios.

Phase 5 — Sensitivity sweep

Purpose: the actual deliverable — re-run Phases 3–4 over a parameter grid to map tipping points and surfaces.

Inputs: the axes in sweep.yaml (ASCbarge, ASCrail, driver VOT, lock scenario). The whole sweep runs twice — once per VOR anchor.

Output — sweep_results.parquet: one mode-share vector per grid cell.

Phase 6 — Policy levers

Beyond the forward sweep, the run emits two policy deliverables per commodity band:

  • min_lever_table.parquet — the minimum ASC_barge (subsidy-equivalent) to reach a target barge share, with a feasibility flag (inverse solver / bisection).
  • min_effort_levers.parquet — least-effort combination of reduced-form levers (Δcost/Δtime/Δσ) and queue-coupled physical levers (added berths + subsidy), with a go/no-go feasibility filter.
Section 5

Configuration reference

Every numeric value lives in config/parameters.yaml with an inline provenance comment; code never embeds numbers. Full parameter detail below.

run / corridor / fidelity

KeyDefaultMeaning
run.rng_seed42master seed (reproducibility)
run.output_dir / processed_dirdata/outputs · data/processedoutput locations
corridor.namelower_mississippi_nola_memphisselects corridor geography (also tennessee_river_paducah_knoxville)
corridor.hub_tazTN_MEMPHIS_HUBdestination hub
fidelity.profilepracticepractice | art | custom — see Fidelity

phase2 — rates, speeds, dwell, availability

KeyDefaultProvenance
truck_rate_per_mile2.30ATRI 2024 Operational Costs of Trucking
barge_bulk_rate_per_ton_mile0.008USACE WCSC / TTI modal comparison
barge_intermodal_rate_per_ton_mile0.020containerized barge
rail_bulk_rate_per_ton_mile0.042STB Carload Waybill 2023 bulk avg
rail_intermodal_rate_per_ton_mile0.065BTS NTS intermodal rail avg
bulk_lift_per_ton7.0grain elevator / coal spout (USACE/USDA)
intermodal_lift_per_ton42.0NCFRP/BTS container lift ($30–55)
truck_speed_mph55.0interstate w/ hours-of-service
barge_speed_mainstem_mph6.0USACE mainstem
rail_speed_intermodal_mph25.0BNSF/CSX reported
barge_hub_dwell_hr8.0NCFRP typical port dwell (origin side under queue mechanism)
rail_hub_dwell_hr12.0NCFRP 11 intermodal-yard dwell
barge_max_origin_drayage_mi150.0barge availability threshold (locked decision #1)
rail_max_origin_drayage_mi75.0rail availability threshold (locked decision #2)

phase25 — reliability

KeyDefaultMeaning
reliability_mechanismterminal_queueterminal_queue | river_events | both
reliability_metrics_emitted[std, buffer95]both emitted; Phase 3 picks one
n_lockage_runs1000MC realizations for the event path
terminal_queue.arrival_rate_per_hr0.25baseline tow arrivals at the hub
terminal_queue.service_rate_per_hr0.1667per-berth rate (~6 h/tow)
terminal_queue.servers2berths/cranes — the investment lever
terminal_queue.congestion_reference_ton_share0.45MSA anchor (SOTA endogenous congestion)
msa.max_iter / tol_arrival_rate30 / 0.002congestion fixed-point damping
lock_delay_distributions.*lognormalper-event/per-lock baseline/improved/degraded (LMR low-water/fog/tow; 9 TN-River locks)

phase3 — behavioral parameters

KeyDefaultProvenance / note
vot_driver_per_hr37.50USDOT 2016 guidance × BLS May-2024 wages; range 30–45
inventory_holding_rate_annual0.15Baumol-Vinod; band 0.10–0.20; swept
supply_delay_cost_per_hr8.0value-independent vessel/demurrage cost; analyst est; swept
carrier_reliability_ratio (γ)0.5carrier reliability (M2); band 0.2–0.8
truck_time_cv0.0truck transit variability (0 = deterministic in demo)
logit_scale_theta (θ)300.0$/util; swept assumption, not tuned
vor_anchors.nchrp_824.reliability_ratio3.0high VOR/VOT bracket (NCHRP 824)
vor_anchors.shams_2017.reliability_ratio0.4low VOR/VOT bracket (de Jong 2014 / Shams 2017)
asc_truck0.0reference alternative
asc_barge_central / asc_rail_central-80 / -80residual unobserved preference, $/shipment (M1/M2); most-swept
asc_barge_by_commodity / asc_rail_by_commodityper-bandM3 (SOTA) commodity-specific ASCs
shipment_size_coef20.0M4 (SOTA) size-economy term
mixed_logit.n_draws / random_coefficients100 / 3 coeffsmixed-logit (M6) heterogeneity

phase4 / phase5 / figures

KeyDefaultMeaning
phase4.n_mc_runs_central100MC runs for variance bands
phase4.use_mc_in_sweepfalseanalytic shares in the sweep (fast)
phase5.n_parallel_workers1vectorized NumPy; single process is fine
figures.dpi / palette300 / amber·blue·greentruck/barge/rail colors

Scenarios & sweep

scenarios.yaml bundles parameter overrides. Built-in: baseline, improved_locks, degraded_locks, hub_investment, trucking_cost_shock, tn_lock_improvement, tn_lock_degradation. sweep.yaml defines the grid axes (asc_barge, asc_rail, beta_time_scale, lock_scenario).

Section 6

Data & inputs

What each phase needs, where it comes from in the SOP vs SOTA path, and how to bring your own data. Full source list: DATA_PATHS.md and white-paper Appendix B.

Per-phase sources

PhaseInputDemo / SOP sourceFunded / SOTA source
1County→TAZ OD flowsIHS Transearch via the team fdt/RES2019-14 pipeline; open fallback BTS FAF5 countySynthFirm firm synthesis
1payload / value densityCensus VIUS 2021; CFS value-per-ton (or Transearch value)SynthFirm shipment sizes
2networks & geometrysynthetic stylized networkBTS NTAD + USACE NTAD shapefiles
2ratesATRI · USACE WCSC · TTI/NWF · USDA AMS · STB Public-Use Waybill · NCFRP 11STB Confidential Waybill / tariffs
2.5delay distributionspublished-literature lognormals + USACE CorpsLocks summariesraw USACE LPMS; NOAA AIS validation
3/4VOT, VOR, θUSDOT 2016 · NCHRP 824 · de Jong 2014 / Shams 2017; θ sweptTN primary stated-preference survey

IHS Transearch vs FAF — and why FAF is not an independent check

IHS Transearch (TDOT-licensed, county-native, carries value) is the preferred primary Phase-1 input; the team's deployed fdt/RES2019-14 pipeline disaggregates it to TAZ-level OD by commodity, mode, tons, and value.

FAF is the open fallback, not an independent validator. FAF's native zones are coarse (132 zones; smallest a county, largest a whole state), and its county-level product is experimental and modeled — an OLS composite of three disaggregation estimates, tons-only, 2022, no value, five commodity groups. Critically, FAF (and that county product) ingest the same STB Waybill (rail) and USACE waterborne (water) series that Transearch uses, so FAF cannot independently cross-check a Transearch-fed mode share for rail/water. The closest-to-observed barge figure is USACE WCSC reported tonnage. (Current series FAF5.7.1; FAF6 ~mid-2026.)

Bring your own data PLANNED — ingest adapter

The Phase-1 input contract is small and stable: origin_taz, destination_taz, sctg2, annual_tons (+ optional value). To run on real flows today you transform your file to that schema and point phase1.disagg_outputs_path at it.

The Phase-1 ingest adapter (planned) will read the real fdt/Transearch output format directly — columns origin_taz, destination_taz, sctg_2d, co_mode, tons, valuee — handling quote-wrapping, external "-" destinations, the SCTG2→band crosswalk, and value density from valuee. It also needs a full corridor file with barge lanes plus TN TAZ centroids; the first received sample (Outbound2040_sample.csv) is a one-county, single-mode format sample only. See Roadmap.

Section 7

Outputs & interpretation

What each output file means and how to read the figures. Numbers below are the current synthetic Lower-Mississippi demo.

Output files

FileGrainKey columns
mode_sharescenario × anchor × modeshare_mean, share_std, ton_share, value_share
mode_share_by_commodity+ commodity bandper-band shares (the RQ1 commodity decomposition)
sweep_resultsgrid cell × anchor × modeanalytic share per parameter combination
min_lever_tablecommodity bandthreshold, feasible (min ASC_barge for target share)
min_effort_leverscommodity bandreduced-form + physical (berths/subsidy) least-effort levers
utility / choicesshipment-leveldiagnostics

Hero figures

Reading the shares

Report ton-share for corridor capacity questions and value-share for economic-exposure questions — they differ sharply because bulk is heavy-but-cheap and manufactured is light-but-valuable. share_std is the Monte-Carlo variance band; treat any difference smaller than ~2× share_std as noise.

Section 8

Fidelity profiles (SOP / SOTA)

One simulator, two depths, selected by fidelity.profile. The named profiles use only implemented cells, so both run end-to-end on the same corridor.

Dimensionpractice (SOP)art (SOTA)Funded-work cell
shipment_generationdirectdirectfirmtype_fame FUNDED
utility_specM2M4—
choice_modelanalyticmixed_logit—
reliability_sourcepublished_lognormalpublished_lognormallpms_fitted FUNDED
parameter_sourcetransferredtransferredtn_survey FUNDED
sweep_resolutioncoarsefine—
mode_availabilitythresholdthresholdgeo_derived PLANNED
networkssyntheticsyntheticntad_real PLANNED
congestion_feedbacknonemsa—
practice and art differ in model richness and compute — they use the same demo data. The richer data cells (real networks, LPMS, survey, SynthFirm) are separate funded-work interfaces and raise a clean NotImplementedError until built. Set profile: custom to mix dimensions via fidelity.overrides.
Section 9

Worked example — the LMR demo

A full run of the Lower-Mississippi corridor under profile: practice. All numbers are the current synthetic demo (illustrative, not calibrated).

1 · Run it

python run_demo.py

2 · Corridor mode share (baseline, NCHRP anchor)

26.9%
Truck · ton-share
40.6%
Barge · ton-share
32.6%
Rail · ton-share

Note the value-share flips: truck carries only ~27% of tons but ~56% of value — the high-value manufactured goods ride the truck.

3 · By commodity (baseline barge ton-share)

BandBargeRailTruckReading
ag_bulk50.8%34.2%15.1%bulk → water/rail compete
mineral_bulk50.7%33.8%15.5%bulk
pet_chem41.8%44.9%13.3%mid-value bulk
build_mfg1.5%2.3%96.2%manufactured → truck dominates
fin_mfg3.0%7.6%89.3%manufactured

4 · The supply lever (barge ton-share by scenario)

ScenarioBargevs baseline
improved_locks (added berth + reliability)45.8%+5.2 pp
baseline40.6%—
degraded_locks (congestion + drought)27.3%−13.3 pp

Supply-side conditions move corridor barge share by ~13 points — the reliability coupling at work.

5 · Policy levers (Phase 6)

Minimum ASC_barge to hold 50% barge ton-share: ag_bulk needs −$95, mineral_bulk −$98, pet_chem +$50 (already past it), while build_mfg and fin_mfg are infeasible at any plausible value. Least-effort to reach 60% barge: bulk bands feasible with ~$124–316/shipment subsidy (or a few hours of time saved); manufactured bands remain infeasible even at maxed levers. Infeasibility is itself the policy finding — some freight will not move at any realistic intervention.

Section 10

Roadmap & planned capabilities

The tool ships a working core plus capabilities behind clean interfaces. This is the forward path; every item is flagged inline elsewhere in the manual with the same badge.

PLANNED near-term, bounded FUNDED-WORK needs restricted data / external integration
CapabilityStatusWhat it addsDepends on
Phase-1 data-ingest adapterPLANNED read a real fdt/Transearch disaggregate file straight into the Phase-1 schema (quote handling, "-" externals, SCTG2→band crosswalk, value density) full corridor file with barge lanes + TN TAZ centroids. Input contract is already stable, so this is a bounded add.
Real network skimPLANNED route on real geometry via load_real_*_graph() (BTS NTAD + USACE shapefiles) shapefile pull + TAZ centroids
Geo-derived mode availabilityPLANNED availability from real terminal geography instead of drayage thresholdsNTAD terminals
LPMS-fitted reliability + AIS validationFUNDED-WORK fit lock-delay distributions from raw USACE LPMS; validate transit vs NOAA AISgated LPMS per-event data; AIS pull
FAME / SynthFirm generationFUNDED-WORK firm-synthesis SOTA Phase-1 path (real shipment sizes)SynthFirm integration (FAME_INTEGRATION_PLAN.md)
Ciw / OpenTNSim queue enginesFUNDED-WORK M/G/c terminal + tandem-lock discrete-event reliabilityexternal libs
TN primary-survey parametersFUNDED-WORK replace transferred VOT/VOR/θ with estimated coefficientsstated-preference survey
Multi-corridor / statewideFUNDED-WORK scale beyond a single corridorstatewide flows + networks
Web UI / Databricks deploymentFUNDED-WORK interactive front enddeployment work

A DOT with open data and a laptop runs the SOP rung today; each funded data acquisition lights up a higher rung from the same codebase.

ParameterValueSource
Driver VOT$37.50/hr (30–45)USDOT 2016 guidance × BLS May-2024 wages
VOR ratio (high / low)3.0 / 0.4NCHRP 824 / de Jong 2014 · Shams 2017
Inventory holding rate0.15/yrBaumol-Vinod inventory theory
Truck rate$2.30/miATRI Operational Costs of Trucking 2024

Glossary

  • SOP / SOTA — state-of-practice (fast, open data) / state-of-the-art (rich, survey+cluster) fidelity profiles.
  • ASC — alternative-specific constant: residual unobserved modal preference, $/shipment (not a transfer penalty — transfer is modeled explicitly).
  • VOT / VOR — value of time / value of reliability.
  • θ — logit scale, dollars per util.
  • Ton-share / value-share — modal split weighted by tonnage / by cargo value.
  • SCTG — Standard Classification of Transported Goods commodity code.
  • TAZ — traffic-analysis zone.

Key data-source URLs

  • BTS FAF: bts.gov/faf · BTS NTAD: bts.gov/ntad
  • USACE WCSC: ndc.ops.usace.army.mil/wcsc · LPMS/CorpsLocks: corpslocks.usace.army.mil
  • ATRI: truckingresearch.org · USDOT VOT guidance: transportation.gov · NCHRP 824: trb.org
  • NOAA AIS: marinecadastre.gov · STB Waybill: stb.gov/reports-data/waybill

Companion documents (canonical)

  • white_paper/golias_microsim_whitepaper.tex — source of truth (methodology)
  • DATA_PATHS.md — per-phase data sources, SOP vs SOTA
  • ALGORITHM.md — the generalized-cost choice core
  • START_HERE.md — project map (folders, copies, status)
  • TASK4_FIX_PLAN.md — build history & decisions
